key_departures.impact: 49ers confirmed Aiyuk will not return. His 2026 guarantees (~$27M) were voided in July 2025 after he went AWOL during the season following a torn ACL/MCL in 2024. Release (likely post-June 1 designation) carries ~$13.3M dead money in 2026 and ~$21.2M in 2027. Was the WR1 on the roster in contract but a non-participant in 2025. The hole is real but the situation was already baked in.

Adam Schefter confirmed Jennings has played his last game with the 49ers. The 49ers signed Mike Evans as his de facto replacement, but Jennings was the team's most reliable receiver in 2025 (55 rec, 643 yds, career-high 9 TDs). His market value (~$22.6M/yr per Spotrac) exceeded SF's comfort range. Loss of a proven third-down weapon and red-zone target.

Bourne signed a 2yr/$10M deal with the Cardinals. He was a versatile veteran depth piece who contributed 37 receptions in 2025 including a 142-yard playoff opener vs the Rams. Depth loss, but not a starter-caliber departure.
Moore is among the 49ers' unrestricted free agents. He was a fringe contributor and his departure is a minor depth loss at an already thin position.

Gross-Matos was a key rotational edge rusher for a defense that posted a league-low 20 sacks and 24.9% pressure rate in 2025. His departure further thins an already depleted pass rush corps behind Nick Bosa.
Robinson was among the 49ers' pending free agents. He was a depth/rotational piece behind Christian McCaffrey. Minor loss.

signings.contract_notes: Initially reported as up to $60.4M with incentives (per Schefter/ESPN). Base contract confirmed at 3yr/$42.4M with $16.3M guaranteed per Ari Meirov. Incentives could push total to $60.4M. Effectively a 1-2 year commitment given low guarantee structure; $16.3M in guarantees is modest for a WR1-caliber player. Per Schefter, it is 'really a shorter-term deal than that, but going to be called a 3-year deal for cap purposes.'
signings.analysis: Future Hall of Famer with 11 consecutive 1,000-yard seasons (before 2025 injury). Directly replaces Jennings/Aiyuk as WR1 alongside Ricky Pearsall. However, the contract structure — only $16.3M guaranteed on a 3yr deal — signals the 49ers view this as a 1-2 year bridge, not a true long-term WR1 solution. Evans is 32 and coming off a broken collarbone and hamstring issues in 2025 (played just 8 games). He is a legitimate, high-quality starter but does NOT fully solve WR depth: Brandon Aiyuk is gone, Jennings is gone, and Bourne departed. The room behind Evans and Pearsall is thin (Robinson, Cowing, Watkins). WR remains a premium draft need for WR2/WR3 depth and long-term youth infusion.

Pineiro re-signed to a 4yr/$17M deal with $10M guaranteed before hitting free agency. He went 28-for-29 on field goals in 2025, including six from 50-plus yards, after replacing Jake Moody in Week 2. This locks in the most reliable kicker the 49ers have had since Robbie Gould. Kicker is a non-issue for the foreseeable future. Contract is the 8th-highest among kickers per OverTheCap.

Lowe projects as a swing tackle/backup in San Francisco. He spent 3 seasons with New England primarily as a swing tackle, with 25 career starts over 4 seasons and 4 LT starts in 2025. He is NOT a Trent Williams replacement — he works behind Williams at LT or behind Colton McKivitz at RT. This signing adds necessary line depth amid the Williams contract uncertainty but does not address the critical OT need if Williams departs or misses time. The 2yr/$12M max deal with only $5.75M guaranteed is depth/swing pricing, not starter pricing. OT remains a legitimate need.

signings.contract_notes: Exact financials estimated at veteran long snapper market rate; official terms not publicly disclosed beyond ESPN/49ers.com confirming a 1-year extension.
signings.analysis: Pro Bowl long snapper re-signed on a 1-year extension. Maintains continuity at a specialty position. No positional need impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Evans partially addresses WR1, but the room is still dangerously thin. Aiyuk (cut), Jennings (departed), and Bourne (Cardinals) are all gone. Evans is 32 and injury-prone on a bridge deal; the contract structure confirms the 49ers see this as a 1-2 year bridge, not a long-term fix. Ricky Pearsall is the only other receiver with starter upside, and he missed significant time in 2025 with injuries. The team still desperately needs a WR2 and a developmental WR in the draft. WR remains a Tier 1 priority — potentially the #1 pick need — with emphasis on a young, speedy playmaker to complement Evans and Pearsall.

The 49ers posted a league-low 20 sacks and 24.9% pressure rate in 2025. Nick Bosa is the only proven pass rusher under contract. Yetur Gross-Matos (rotational rusher) is a free agent. No EDGE additions made in free agency as of March 10. Trey Hendrickson was linked to the 49ers but remained unsigned as of Day 1. EDGE remains a critical Tier 1 need — the 49ers must address this through the draft and/or a late-addition FA signing.

Trent Williams' contract situation is unresolved and the 49ers are reportedly open to trading him if a deal cannot be reached. Williams turns 38 in July and carries a ~$39M cap hit. Vederian Lowe (signed) is a depth/swing option, not a Williams replacement. Left guard also remains a need with Aaron Banks long gone and no reliable starter in place. OT stays Tier 2, potentially escalating to Tier 1 if Williams is traded.

Left guard is a confirmed need per ESPN — no starter currently in place. Jake Brendel is returning at center on his existing deal ($3.6M), providing stability there, but the LG spot has no clear answer. Spencer Burford/Jon Feliciano-type additions have not been made. IOL remains a Tier 2 draft/FA need, specifically at LG.

The 49ers ranked 25th in passing yards allowed (3,951) and 21st in passing TDs allowed (29) in 2025. Renardo Green is the CB1 but was a potential trade candidate per ESPN. Charvarius Ward and Talanoa Hufanga both departed in 2025 free agency. No CB additions made in 2026 FA yet. Secondary remains a significant need.
Talanoa Hufanga departed to Denver in 2025 FA on a 3yr/$39M deal. The 49ers have not yet added a replacement starter at safety. S remains a draft/FA target.

Cross-referenced: No EDGE signed in FA. Trey Hendrickson (17.5 sacks, linked to SF) did not sign with the 49ers on Day 1. League-low 20 sacks in 2025 makes EDGE the most critical positional need alongside WR. Must be addressed in rounds 1-3 of the draft.

George Kittle tore his Achilles in January 2026 and may miss significant time in 2026. Jake Tonges is the backup. Luke Farrell (signed in 2025) provides a veteran option. Kittle's injury keeps TE in the draft conversation for rounds 3-5 as insurance.

key_additions.headline: Future Hall of Famer and 6x Pro Bowler replaces Aiyuk/Jennings as WR1 on a team-friendly bridge deal
Re-signed before free agency opened; went 28-of-29 on FGs in 2025, ending years of kicker instability
Depth swing tackle adds OL insurance amid unresolved Trent Williams contract standoff
Pro Bowl long snapper returns on a one-year extension to maintain special teams continuity

key_losses.contract_summary: Cut (post-June 1 designation expected); ~$13.3M dead cap in 2026
key_losses.headline: Former All-Pro WR officially done in SF after going AWOL in 2025; guarantees voided, leaving massive dead cap and WR hole
49ers' most reliable 2025 receiver (9 TDs, clutch third-down specialist) expected to cash out on open market
Versatile veteran slot/perimeter option departs to division rival Cardinals, further thinning SF's WR depth
Key rotational pass rusher departs, deepening SF's EDGE crisis after league-low 20-sack 2025 season

situational_flags.trent_williams_status: UNRESOLVED — As of March 10, the 49ers and Williams have not reached a contract agreement. The 49ers are reportedly open to a trade. Williams carries a ~$39M cap hit with a $10M option bonus due March 20. If Williams is traded or released, OT escalates to Tier 1 critical and becomes the top positional need in the draft.
situational_flags.george_kittle_health: Kittle tore his Achilles in January 2026 and may miss significant time in 2026. TE depth (Jake Tonges, Luke Farrell) is concerning. Kittle's availability will shape draft strategy at TE.
situational_flags.nick_bosa_contract: Bosa has 3 years remaining with a $41.62M cap hit in 2026. A restructure could free up to $17M additional cap space. Status: Not yet restructured as of March 10.
situational_flags.brandon_aiyuk_release_timing: Official release not yet executed as of March 10 (league year opens March 11). Post-June 1 designation expected to split dead cap: ~$13.3M in 2026 / ~$21.2M in 2027.
situational_flags.pass_rush_urgency: SF posted a league-low 20 sacks in 2025. Trey Hendrickson was available on Day 1 of FA but did not sign with SF. EDGE is co-equal with WR as the team's biggest positional need heading into the draft.

notes: Free agency officially opens March 11, 2026 at 4pm ET. All signings above were agreed to during the legal tampering window (March 9-10) and are confirmed by ESPN, NFL Network, and Spotrac. Mike Evans contract base figures confirmed by Ari Meirov / The 33rd Team ($42.4M base, $16.3M gtd) vs. initial Schefter report of up to $60.4M with incentives — both figures used in analysis. Jennings and Aiyuk departures are confirmed in intent but not yet official transactions as of March 10. Trent Williams situation is fluid and unresolved.
